title: Thi Thuy Dung (Jun) Nguyen
text:
Jun is an Assistant Professor of Accounting at NHH Norwegian School of Economics, Norway. She holds a Ph.D. in Accounting from Universidad Carlos III de Madrid, Spain.
Her primary area of interest is archival auditing. Her current research considers how regulatory information, market incentives, and auditor communication shape auditor behavior and audit market outcomes. She is also interested in advanced econometrics and frontier data science methods in auditing research, including advancing her existing knowledge of textual data analysis and machine learning methods.
She loves cooking, traveling, building Lego and drawing in Procreate.

title: Research
text:
Audit in the Dark: Auditor Responses to Risk in the Absence of Authoritative Guidance (with Beatriz García Osma, Nhan Ha, and W. Robert Knechel)
 Auditor Consequences of Opinion Selling (with Beatriz García Osma and Encarna Guillamon-Saorin)

 Audit Materiality and Debt Contracting (with Beatriz García Osma, Encarna Guillamon-Saorin, and Omar De Ines Anton)

title: Personal
text:
TravelHobbies


I’d love to travel and explore the diverse local cultures and cuisines around the world. Being in academia not only enables me to journey across the globe but also give me a chance to meet new people from various countries and cultural backgrounds. Here is an interactive map showing the footprints of my journeys thus far!








Can you perceive the aroma of food wafting from these photos? I enjoy cooking and having culinary experiences at various restaurants everywhere I travel with my closed friends. Another hobby I love is creating art in Procreate and watching time-lapse videos to see the process of how things are made. I also make time for Zumba fitness and swimming to maintain a healthy work-life balance.
